articles.headline: SK Hynix CEO Warns of Worst Memory Supply Shortage in 2027, With Demand Outstripping Supply Into 2030
articles.summary: SK Hynix CEO forecasts the global memory industry will face its worst-ever supply shortage in 2027, with demand exceeding production capacity well into the next decade despite aggressive expansion. This shortage will likely drive up cloud costs, AI infrastructure expenses, and hardware pricing for businesses relying on chips and memory.

articles.headline: Apple Sues OpenAI Over Alleged Trade Secret Misappropriation in Consumer Hardware Push
articles.summary: Apple filed a lawsuit against OpenAI and two former employees, alleging theft of trade secrets to accelerate OpenAI's entry into consumer hardware. The case escalates tensions between the companies and could delay hardware launches, impact licensing deals, and increase legal/compliance risks for AI firms developing physical devices.

articles.headline: China Blocks Helium Exports Amid Iran War Escalation, Threatening Global Chipmaking Supply Chains
articles.summary: China temporarily blocked helium exports, a critical gas for chip manufacturing, as the Iran war disrupts global supply. This action risks semiconductor production delays, higher chip prices, and supply chain instability for businesses dependent on memory, processors, and AI hardware.
